title: Don’t Quit - How To Get Ahead Of 99% Of Personal Brands
author: Dan Koe

sections.title: Why You're Stuck At 0
sections.content: If you are a creative, or a writer or a musician, or someone who wants to become a creator,this could be the most important video that you watch this year.Because of this number 3 to 6 months.That's how long most creators are trapped in beginner hell.Beginner hell is that dreadful place, where you feel like you don't know what you're doing.You question whether it's worth it to keep going.You're thinking of what you're going to try next just to do it all over again.And unfortunately, most people quit before things start to get good.But understand this most people think the answer is to, just be consistent, to keep pushing through, even when you aren't making progress.Zero likes, zero followers, zero sales.It's one of the most painful things in the world,because becoming a creator was supposed to be your ticket, into the good life, or was it all just a scam?Was it a pipe dream that was sold to you by marketers and gurus?Now here's the brutal truth. you aren't making progress because you aren't doing the right things.So before you go back to the life that you swore you would leave behind.Listen to what I have to say.You need to reach the point where everything clicks.You need that moment where clarity strikes, and you know exactly how to break through, to intermediate heaven.Now I know you're on the edge right now.You don't want to keep going.You don't know how to keep going.But remember why you started.You know you want to do something creative.You know you need an audience to do that outside of a job.You know you want to be in control of the money, you make in the lifestyle you design.You know that in order to do those things, you need to write or create.You need to become your own media company, that attracts people to your independent work,your self-published books, your music, your ideas, your expertise.You just need to reach that glorious moment, the click,and you will finally feel in control of your future.

sections.title: How To Escape Beginner Hell
sections.content: Jeff Bezos said that all overnight success takes about ten years.So that's point number one is just stop, comparing yourselves to people who have been in the game for 5 to 10 years,and you think that you should somehow just match their skill, and their results after one month of learning from them.Because here's the thing is that you have to hammer this into your brain.This has to be just automatic thought process for you, is that, there is a series of actions you can take to get the results you want.There is there is a series of actions. They're out there.The ones you're taking right now aren't those series of actions.So you need to experiment and find them plain and simple.But most creators just follow blindly and mindlessly, what they're told to do by myself, and by other people who are teaching things like writing and social media.But that's the problem.It's not that these people are giving out bad advice,it's that this isn't a job.This isn't something with a linear. set of tasks that you can just perform every single day.Shut your mind off.Don't think, don't be creative and live like you always have.This is a one person business.You are the CEO, you are the CMO.You are the CEO.You are everything.Please start acting like it.Now I know this because I was in beginner, hell for almost five years, not just 3 to 6 months.I jump between dropshipping, three ecommerce stores,digital art and photography of Facebook Ads agency and SEO content agency,and eventually had to get a job in web design, because the loans were piling up and I wasn't willing to drown any longer.Now at my job, I procrastinated on my work to make freelance web design work.When I started writing on social media to learn more clients,I didn't experience a beginner hell, because I knew how to avoid it.There was a select few skills that I learned along the way, through all of those failures that led to my own click, my own, moment, my own oh, I can actually control this. Oh, it is actually skill and not luck.I'll tell you what those are.That's what this entire video is about to give you.The mini masterclass of the exact things that actually get results, in growth, follower sales, whatever it may be, brand authority,all of those things.So here's a brief overview of what we're going to talk about.First is a different way to think about finding your niche.Second is the best type of content to bring in followers.Third is how to monetize without relying on platform ad revenue.So for those that want to start on YouTube just to get paid, by YouTube, we're not here to do that in fourth.Why you should forget building a funnel.Because social media has changed and much more in between,because it's not as simple as a bullet point list.If I were to just tell you,build an audience and build a product that the audience wants,nobody would get there.You already know that you're missing the ideas that form a web in your mind. And once that web is complete, or that puzzle is complete,or that picture is complete and it's clear, then you know what to do.But just having like a blurry outline of the picture,you're not going to get anywhere.In other words,I'm going to tell you how to end up like this guy who I saw on Twitter recently.Props to you, Sir Jackson, where you can just see the exponential graph.It's like negative followers, a little bit of followers, and then boom, out of nowhere, just a bunch.That is what I call an exponential event.That's what we want to make happen and then replicate over and over again.Now I've personally grown to over 3.8, probably, close to 4 million followers across all different platforms now.And I've also taught this to over 30,000 people I haven't checked with.The last number was, but it's over 30,000, which is a lot.So here are the seven tips, strategies and insights, that will change everything for you.

sections.title: 1) Do What Works For Beginners
sections.content: Number one, extremely important do what works for beginners, because here's the thing, beginner hell can last for three months or 30 years, right?30 years. That's a starving artist.You do your art,you never think about the business side, and you're never able to actually sustain that thing.You're never able to sustain your life's work.The creators who end up in that spot,who can just never make progress, seem, to be trying to do what the advanced creators are doing, without realizing that that's not what the advanced creators did.Beginners start posting philosophical quotes, or try to be the next Marcus Aurelius.When nobody knows who they are or cares about what they do,they have no authority.And I hate to break it to you, but you aren't a Roman emperor.You aren't respected because nobody knows who you are.That means if you want to take social media as the path, to building your life's work, which is a possibility now,you have to build authority on social media. You don't just start posting like you're the most important person in the world.You have to build up to actually being respected by a group of people.So how do you build authority?What are the what's the thing that beginners should do?The first is to just solve more problems.What I mean by that is 80% of your content as a beginner, should all be focused around a pain point.They should all start with a pain point.I've talked about this so many times, but I know this from experience as well.Not just calling you out.I'm calling myself out when I sit down to write,I have an idea and then I'm like, okay, I'm going to write it out and I'm like,okay, yeah, that looks good.That sounds good.I like it, but then it doesn't perform as well as I thought it would.What I need you to do and what I need to continue to do, with myself, it's a process of programing your mind.To think articulately is to.When you have an idea, think of the pain point immediately.How does it help people?What's the value? Whenever you're writing content,recording a real recording a YouTube video to start with the pain point,something that people can relate with.Now how do you actually start this thing?I want you the next time you write, content or create content, start with these words or phrases.First. Is most people example.Most people suck at writing.Second is if you struggle with example is if you struggle, to focus for more than five minutes.The third is the worst example, the worst skill you can learn is web design.My best content that actually captures attention and deliver, some value to people.Hits on a pain point or a problem.Problems capture attention.Problems open the curiosity loop.Problems make it 100 times easier to make the rest of the post impactful.Seriously, if you don't know what to write, just find any idea.Any idea in this video. Write it down.Think of a pain point and then after that, it's, pretty simple to finish off the writing. Write. If it's a problem, then you give a list of steps to overcome it.So most people suck at writing.Do this, do this, do this, do this, boom.Done. It's valuable.Or you can have a unique perspective or novel insight.So most people suck at writing because why?So if you need just a framework for writing content pain point process.So step by steps then pain point why or pain point insight.So there's bullet list content or there's pain point followed by why.Why should you solve that thing.So that's tip number one for beginners.Tip number two is to cover the most important ideas, in depth under your brand because you need to become an authority.So once you pick a topic to write, about for your creator business or whatever you're building,you don't just start writing random ideas.You can.We're going to talk about that a bit later with make Noise and Focus on signal,but you have to understand that there are a select few, evergreen ideas that almost every single brand. that talk about a specific topic, talk about your job,is to find out what those are and talk about them under your brand, so that you have this, library of information, for people who follow you to dig into and see you as an authority.Think of it like writing a small scale book on a specific topic, to become an authority about it all over your brand, in all kinds of content.You just need the pillars there, the fundamentals.So in something like building a business,those evergreen ideas are things like how to choose a niche,the best business model for beginners,the best skills to learn, or how to make X amount of money in Y amount of time.There's a reason you see these all the time and why they always do well.If you try to avoid these because you want to be unique, and you want to be all these authenticity, uniqueness,whatever, without the fundamentals that actually help people, and are the starting point for everyone, getting into the topic that you talk about,you're going to fail. As I said, artist or art in business, they need to crossover.There needs to be a blend of both.Those topics also work because nobody gets sick of the fundamentals.And as your audience grows, the new audience, hasn't seen that old content, so it needs to be on repeat.I posted a video last week about how I made 6.9 million, writing for two hours a day, and it's a video, I posted before I just rerecorded it.There's a reason behind that.Someone commented like, this is just repetitive.I'm leaving, I'm unsubscribing, and that's fine.That's what you're supposed to do.You're not supposed to follow me all the time, and just continue consuming the content.You're supposed to do something with it.And if it's being repeated,does that not signal to you that it's an important thing to do?Now, a lot of other people, they like the repetition.I know you do because you need it.That's how you condition your mind to do something different.That's how you change your behavior. People need to be reminded more than they need something new.Get used to that.As someone who is trying to give value now, once, you know what topic you want to write about,research 5 to 10 accounts that talk about that thing.Notice what content they repeat on a weekly or monthly basis,and notice what content they all talk about.Then make it a consistent part of your brand.Create multiple free guides around those topics.Create content about one of them at least once a day.Write short, medium, and long form content posts, threads, carousels, newsletters,and the rest.For those asking what a carousel is, it's on Instagram, where you can swipe through multiple posts.It's like ten posts in one swipe, a little carousel.Now this is a point in and of itself for later in the video,but you need to build a world that people can sink their teeth into.You need your own small scale Netflix that people can binge, watch or binge read. Now.Step number three for doing. what works as a beginner is to just filter your favorite accounts, for their oldest content, because chances are you're, probably just copying feel good content from the accounts, you follow. They already have an audience.They already have a baseline level of engagement.People already know who they are.So what we're trying to do here, is we're trying to study what that account created in order to grow.So on YouTube, you go to their profile and then you filter by all this content.Now you're not just going to take all of the oldest content.You're going to look through and look through them all,and you're going to see, okay, which one, which one created that spike.Because as you'll find, social media growth comes in waves.It's not just linear.It like kind of stalls out like it was for me for like four videos, in a row where normally I'd get like 150, 200,000 views, and then both a combination of the ideas,not just just not being very good and just how social media goes in general. I hit multiple where it's like 50,000 views, 60,000 views for like four videos,and it's demotivating for a lot of people.That's where most people fall off because they're like,oh, the YouTube algorithm changed and this and that.It's like, no, dude, you just need to iterate.So the point with that is find their, all this content scroll all the way to the bottom.If it takes an hour to get to the bottom of someone's content, do it.Scroll up and just copy paste into cortex or something like that,where you copy paste over the content that started to do well for them.That was an anomaly.And then you use that as a framework.

sections.title: 2) Your Content Isn't That Good (and The 10 Koemmandments)
sections.content: We'll talk about how to do that.Now.Number two, for how to escape beginner hell,is that your content just isn't that good.Because if you weren't growing, you're doing something wrong.And once you're honest with yourself about that, you can actually change it.You can't just pick a topic and start writing.You need to take the reader psychology into account.You need to become a dopamine dealer.You need to capture attention, hold attention, and deliver value.Most people think they're doing this, but they aren't.They write a post and send it without any second thought or questioning, whether it's actually good.All of those things capturing attention, holding attention, etc.require a lifetime of study within themselves.Now, what we're about to go over is extremely important.This is like the Ten Commandments of Engagement is what I call them,or the Ten Commandments of impactful writing.If you want, you can download cortex on desktop,and if you press option C, it will open up a capture modal. As you're watching this video.So you can do both at the same time.You can watch and take notes.So what you would do in that case is, you would create a new document titled YouTube Video Notes,and then you would go into capture and you would type at YouTube video notes,so you can connect to that page, and then you start taking ideas there.And then they're all categorized.It's like texting yourself ideas.So the first commandment of engagement is specific numbers.And with all of these, these are just, little things, that you can sprinkle throughout your content, to capture attention or hold attention.That's important because you're competing on social media.You're competing with dumb memes or funny memes and hot people.You need to stand out in another click that you have to make is like, hey,there is something that I can write.There's something that I can put right here, that will stop there, scroll and deliver something valuable.That's the creative challenge. So what you're doing here is specific numbers in the headline.It's like, okay, here's seven steps to achieve desired outcome.Or Apple just released a $1,175 iPhone, whatever.Now this also ties in with number two, which is pattern interrupts.So pattern interrupt, is something that breaks them out of their conditioned process, of what they're doing.So on social media they're scrolling through, they're seeing on Twitter,they're seeing like blocks of tweets.They're seeing, on Instagram, people talking to the camera, or whatever it may be.You need something that is different and that stops the scroll.Now, there's multiple ways to do that that we'll talk about,but in general, you could do a visual pattern interrupt, so you can have something on the screen, that makes them be like, okay, what the heck? Just went on.The specific number is something that most people probably aren't doing.So that's a pattern interrupt as well.The third thing very powerful is negativity bias, because the human brain is wired to pay attention to and remember negativity.Now, this doesn't mean that you have to be negative.It just means that you should try spinning, some of your writing in a negative light.An example could be you weren't going to achieve great, things compared to you will never hit rock bottom again.The second point is a lot more potent, but it's just switched.The framing is switched.You're going to achieve great things.It's like, okay, cool, thank you.You're never going to hit rock bottom again.Pain point problem very potent.Commandment number four is just a target call out.So you're calling out who you're talking to.And this doesn't have to be a specific target audience.Like oh hey calling all coaches that sell a $10,000 offer or more.I have a lead generation system for you that will bring you to $50,000 a month.It doesn't have to be like that.It could be as simple as if you're in your 20s, or calling all creators or creatives or musicians. or something where you're not even calling them out,you're just talking about them.So fathers are a gift to mankind, as an example, could save mothers.You're just calling out a specific group of people or talking to them.You can take one idea and you can tell it to another person,to the people in your audience, and it takes a completely new shape.Commandment number five is problem.Call out, in other words, what we talked about.Just use more pain points.Now number six is the opposite of that.So the potential benefit, because all good content implies a transformation a story.You have the pain point.What's the benefit of overcoming it.You have to answer the question what's in it for me?If you read through the content that you wrote, and it doesn't answer what's in it for me fast,then they're not going to pay attention.Some people will, some people won't.That's just the nature of social media. It's not 0 or 1.It's in between.Commandment number seven is social proof. because when you flex your results, experience, or credentials, people assume, that you know something they don't, and they want to continue reading or following.Now, Justin Welsch is someone who's great at doing this without being cocky.I remember a post of his that I actually saved where it says,sometimes you need to ask people to buy your product.If you don't, you're missing out on 12 x days.Simple as that.And he had a screenshot of him hitting like $468,000 in a month.And it's like, you don't have to be cocky.You don't have to be like, I made $8 billion in five seconds.Here's exactly what you should do.And everyone else is wrong on the Justin well side of things.He's like my little one person business hit this revenue goal.It's pretty cool.So not only are people now seeing him as an authority,as someone they can learn from,but he's being humble in the process because the thing here is, just talk about your results.Because I can't compete with results.It doesn't get results for you. You're I can't lose 20 pounds for you.It can tell you how, maybe, but you're the one who did it.So whether you're a writer who just finished, the first chapter of their book, share it.Share what you do.Share what you accomplished,even if it's a small goal because you're not trying to attract.You're not trying to prove anything here.You're just trying to show that you're doing something because most people don't, doesn't have to be around money.It's just any goal.Even if you read five pages of a new book, just share it now.Commandment number eight for higher engagement.Extremely important.This one is will change everything for you is just confidence and conviction.Now this is my favorite point because a lot of people just refuse, to have conviction in their beliefs.They say, well, oh, nothing's black or white, nothing's absolute. And I get it.You just read a book on metaphysics and it I get it.But you're not doing this to be factually correct.You're doing this to be metaphorically impactful. If the other person can't read through the lines of your writing,if they're reading literally, not metaphorically to get the point.The writing isn't about the literal words on the page.It's about the energy.It's about the point that people come to in their own minds.So to do this,just eliminate words that imply uncertainty, speak in absolutes, when possible, and exaggerate your point to add energy.And of course, do not abuse these for the sake of engagement.So as an example,rather than saying it may be wise if some people develop their skill set,say it is crucial that everyone on planet Earth develops their skill set.And I would argue that that's pretty true most of the time.So an example for me is a previous tweet, I had which said how to get ahead of 99% of people,go quiet for three months, laser in on one big goal.Self educate like mad.Apply everything you learn.Fail as much as possible every month.Take a break and have some fun bursts of intensity, or how you blow past everyone else. Now, I wrote that post almost five years ago, now, and I'm sure you've heard that headline everywhere.How to get ahead of 99% of people.It's my most popular video now because what I did, is I noticed, hey, this post for me got a lot of likes.It brings in the most followers I've ever had.It brought in like 600 when I was a beginner.Now if you copy that, if you just copy paste that tweet and post it over,you may get some engagement.I'm not telling you to do that,but you're probably going to be disappointed, because you're still taking shortcuts.But because that piece of content did well,I turned it into a thread which did extremely well.I turned that thread into a YouTube video, and that is my top YouTube video now, with over 1.6 million views.Commandment number nine is punch, rhythm and readability.So when you're writing, do this.Start short into the point.Then, just like this, continue on with your idea.Use line breaks. People love bullet points.You can get as creative as you'd like. Do you notice how I'm guiding you down the page or through the video?Do you notice how I start short, keep you engaged and deliver a hint of value?Even the bullet point list is ordered from the shortest point to longest.Now last commandment number ten is warnings or cautionary advice.So let's read this post.Be careful telling people your goals.It releases dopamine similar to actually achieving them.Skip the instant gratification.Go quiet and build.So I got an idea from a book, and then I started, by warning people about a trap and then telling them why.This is an incredible way to write content.Because every topic has an entire list of traps that people can fall into.So by giving those warnings,you position yourself as someone who knows what they're talking about.Now, you don't, and shouldn't use all ten commandments that we just talked about at once,but I would encourage you to have a list of them open as you write,so you can write something and then refer back and like, okay, how can I make this better?If you want extra help with this,I this came from to our writer my writing and content course,and that goes over all of my writing frameworks, newsletter thread, tweets, and how all of those tied together,how I start with a newsletter, break it into a thread, breaking into tweets.Super easy to write content each week.

sections.title: 3) Start With Training Wheels
sections.content: Now.Step number three for escaping beginner hell is to start with training wheels.So important because most people don't have an idea problem.They have a structure problem.Any idea can go viral if and only if you structure it the right way.Just like there's a series of steps, you can take to achieve anything you want in life,there is a series of sentences or a structure that makes any idea go viral.That's the entire fun, right?You have an idea and you're like, oh,I don't think this will do well on social media.You can structure it, right?You can rewrite that idea in a way that does do well.That's like when you practice that and fail at that so many times,that's how you become a master creator.The thing here is that generating ideas isn't hard,because you just pass by at least 50 in this video, so far that you could take and run with.Then the objection there is like, well, Dan, I don't know all of this stuff.Like I, I didn't come up with this myself. You don't have to talk as if you came up with it.You just share the idea.So the thing with that is that you can turn even the worst idea, into something that people want to read, watch, or listen to.You can tell a story.So how do you practice structure writing structure?First thing you can do is to create a swipe file.If you want my swipe file, inside of cortex that you can duplicate over and open as you write content.If you don't know how to write content cortex go to the cortex YouTube video.I know I'm promoting cortex a lot, but this is literally what it was made for.And it's free and mobile is going out soon and it's sick.Our goal with mobile is to make it faster than Apple Notes,to be able to capture an idea,because that's like a big hurdle for us is getting people over from Apple Notes,because it's just ingrained into their brain. Right.We have to play the psychology game.But with this, if you want to be a creator,then you have to keep your finger on the pulse of the market. You need to study.You need to scroll the timeline as a researcher, not a consumer.You need to scroll to hunt for ideas.And as you're scrolling,you see one that's good and like, damn, I want that under my brand.Just like the evergreen ideas that you have to write under your brand.Your brand is an amalgamation of the best ideas that you think are the best.It's not about you taking one idea and then your brand is known as that.It's all of the combination of ideas that you post over time.That's what makes you different.It's not just one piece of content, it's all of them, over the course of 6 to 12 months that you stack up.So when you see one of these posts, you copy the link,you paste it in cortex capture or in cortex itself and embed it as a tweet.So let's go back to that post as an example and just break it down.So how to get ahead of 99% of people.Go quiet for three months, laser in on one big goal.Self educate like mad. Apply everything you learn.Fail as much as possible every month. Take a break and have some fun bursts of intensity, or how you blow past everyone else so you can see a clear structure.You have a broad hook with a desired outcome and specific number.Then you have five exaggerated and polarizing steps in a bullet list.Now, with a bullet list, there's more benefit than it just being readable.Anyone can engage with any given point in the list.They read through it and they're like, nah,I don't agree with that one. Don't agree. Don't care.Oh yeah, that one.I love that one. Like retweet.After that, it has additional context that answers the objection.Well, I can't disappear completely.I have friends and family, and I know this because I've written enough content, to know that was an objection beforehand, so I could add it to the post.And then finally, a last line that ties everything together.So let's take that same structure and let's plug in a new idea.Let's act like I got an idea from the book,and it's like meditation is running for the mind. So I could turn that into something completely new with the same structure,like how to clear your mind in 60s lock yourself in a room, sit in the corner,breathe deep into your belly.Feel the breath with all senses.Repeat for one minute.Any time you get distracted, refocus your attention, on the breath, meditation sprints or how you achieve calm fast.That's a good piece of content.Do you see how I'm not just copying people's tweets?I'm studying the structure, studying, what works, and plugging a five word idea into it.Now, if I didn't want to use that hook, like how to clear your mind in 60s,I could take any of the Ten Commandments of Engagement, that we went over and create a new one,like How to Destroy Negative Thoughts in 60s.So spin it negative.Now with all of this, don't just do this with tweets.You do this with anything.You do this with a newsletter, a landing page for your product.Next video we're going to talk about, how to build and launch your first $100,000 product.As a creator. You can do this with YouTube, videos, with reels, with TikToks, with any of these things,you need a structure open so you can practice, writing scripts or just content with training wheels.And then once you're good or once you can write on your own,then you take them off, but then you start to wiggle a bit,and that's where you learn the most.That's when you really solidify the learning.Now, a second tip for writing with training, wheels is to just study writing frameworks,because when I first started, writing frameworks were a lifesaver.I feel like most people don't do this.They don't write according to a framework.I personally read the book cash for tithing and learn the basics of frameworks, like Ada, which is attention, interest, desire, action.And it's like, think of that.It just makes it so much easier to structure an idea.You're writing all of these ideas, like willy nilly.When you could take to a spa store.You can type in chat, GPT or cortex AI when it comes out and say, hey, give me ten copywriting frameworks to,for short form content and see what it spits out.And then you take those, and you practice writing an idea in all of those frameworks.If you want my own framework,or if you want a document that you can duplicate to cortex,I'm going to give you a bunch during this video, so that you can actually have something in cortex.So it feels like it's all there.But the pain and process framework go link in the description.They're free templates. Just duplicate them.Now tip number three for using training wheels, to enhance your writing is to just have them open as you write.I'm just doubling down on this idea.So capture ideas throughout the week.Sit down 2 to 3 days out of the week for 1 to 2 hours.Open up cortex or another writing or note software.Paste in your content frameworks,open them in a pane on the side and write out your ideas.

sections.title: 4) Nobody Knows Who You Are
sections.content: and reference the frameworks.Now step number four to escaping beginner hell is just, understanding that nobody knows who you are.You have good content.You know your ideas hit hard.You're writing all day, every day,but you still gain maybe a few followers a day.Max. So what's the problem?Nobody knows who you are.You're just waiting for your Lord and Savior.That algorithm to launch your post to the moon when that's not going to happen.And even if it would, you should act like it wouldn't.Your growth is on you.It's in your control if you know what you're doing.Because understand.And this content is only one small piece of the puzzle when it comes to growth.The bigger part of the equation is the amount of people who know you, and like you,because they're more likely to share and engage with your posts.The amount of people who see your posts,because almost nobody will, as a beginner, in the quality of friendships you make with those on the rise,because they'll pull you up as they grow. Another thing to understand is that if you have 500 followers, and you get retweeted or reposted or shared to your story, by someone who has 500,000 followers,your audience is no longer 500.Your audience is 500 and 500,500.Of course, there's some drop off with reposts or story shares, but your audience, is much larger when you get shared by large audiences.This is how people make tens of thousands of dollars with small audiences,because they understand how to generate traffic manually through networking.Beginners think they are limited to their follower count,and the algorithm is how they grow.Experts know they can access their own followers and their networks followers.They write a post they know will bring in followers or sales and have 10 to 15, people share it.This isn't something they do every day because it's strategic.They build up goodwill and act right when the time is right.Sahil bloom is someone,even with over a million followers right. now, is doing this perfectly well because he understands, that his audience is only a small portion, of the people that he has access to.He's launching a book,The Five Types of Wealth, and he's working day and night, to get eyes on those books.But he's not just working day and night on content.He's working day and night on getting other people to share the book.And there's unique ways to do that where he sends the book to them.He marks specific spots in the chapters and he says, hey,if you could share, that would be awesome.Simple as that.I posted it to my Twitter.I just took pictures of the pages, and then that was doing really well, going almost viral.So he told other people to do that.Then it's just taken over Twitter.If you just do that on a small scale, with something like a Twitter thread, then you have a ton of impressions.And if you can't turn a ton of impressions into a replacement, for your salary, I don't know what you're doing.So what what do you do here? One is inject yourself in a tribe, because the most basic, social media advice is to just reply to large accounts.But that's like half baked doesn't really work advice.What you're actually doing here is a few things you're writing.Quality replies not great post or repeating what they said were ChatGPT.So people have a reason to look at your profile and follow you again.Follow the ten commandments of engagement that we talked about.This applies to all writing, not just your posts.The second thing is just becoming well known to a tribe of people.So if I wanted to break into a social media circle, like the startup circle or the tech Circle,I would simply reply to ten great accounts in that space.By adding value and personal experience,the consumers in that space get used to my face, and follow me after they see enough, not after my first reply.When you go on social media, you see one account and you're like,oh yeah, they're friends with this account, this account, this account, it's unconscious to you.But you make these connections to determine who's important in your network.You have to become a part of those connections consistently in this takes time.So the last thing about replies is that your seeding connections,the more you reply and DM people within a certain niche group,the easier it is to DM them or have them DM you.They will engage more with your posts and feed you opportunities.Now this goes into number two which is practicing non needy networking.I have an entire video on this.Go watch it.But it's a.It's a shame that this has to be taught, but it does social media has made us like very socially awkward.Nobody has social skills as a skill anymore, because they weren't taught through school or when they were younger.So you have to teach yourself,because what you don't understand is that social media, social skills are arguably the most important skill, you can learn to succeed there. The thing with this is that people are as afraid of DMing, someone as they are to saying hi to someone on the street.When was the last time you said, hi to someone on the street and sparked up a conversation?Do you think you're going to do it in the DMs?Unless you know how you have to actually learn the skill,but in reality, both of those things are as simple, as finding a shared interest to form the connection around.So here's an example of when Dicky Bush,who had a lot of followers at the time in 2021, reached out to me, because I just posted my own fitness progress, and what I was doing.I was talking about switching back to high carb, and to see how it impacts mental clarity.He asked a few questions and talked about what he was doing,and then I just continued talking. Right.It seemed like a natural conversation because it was a natural conversation.What he did here was he took what I wrote, on Twitter or Instagram or LinkedIn or any of those things,and he just acted as if I was talking to him, and then continued the conversation with me in the DMs, by linking the tweet and then just writing what he thought.And then the conversation flowed naturally.Now, what happens next is that he sends me a thread, that he wrote five days later,and he just sat through this together.After a bit of reflection,I appreciate you bringing the positive energy around here.And then I reposted that right.I had a decent following as well,so I reposted that, and he knew well that I was going to do that.He's just playing a smart social game and there's nothing wrong with that.That's what we're all doing when we're making friends. Now.If you want the non needy networking again, link in the description.I'm just giving you cortex documents so you can reference these things.Now this leads into number three which is planning one strategic post a week.If you aren't trying to get one of your best posts, shared every single week by someone on social media,then you will not grow, even if you will act like you won't. So what you're doing here is you're having conversations every week.You're researching the timeline, not scrolling or consuming,and then you're just talking to people.You're replying to people keeping conversations going since, starting the conversations in the DMs.And then based on those conversations,you have a ton of content ideas that you can write about.Write a post, write a thread.A thread will usually bring in more followers,or a carousel or reel or whatever it is.Send it to the person or people you were talking to, about the same topic and just be like, hey, through this together,thought you'd like it.You can even mention them in it, so that they benefit from it as well and get followers.And they know that.

sections.title: 5) You Need A Product
sections.content: So they're more likely to repost it now.Step number five to escaping beginner.Hell is just you need a product because most creators, wait to monetize until they hit some imaginary follower account.Just to be disappointed when nobody buys,or they think they'll make a decent, income from platform monetization like YouTube, until they hit a wall and their views plummet for two months.The point is, if you want to be in control of your income,you need to sell a product or service because that's what your employer does.That's how you make an income, is you play a role, in the marketing and sales and operations of selling a product.So if you want to start a one person business,you need to build a product or service.Because this also does a few things.This is what makes you an authority.That's the first thing is when you have a product,people start to see you as an authority in that thing.Your engagement increases because you have something worth buying, you have value and people can see that even if they don't buy the product.The thing here is that most creators build, an audience of people that don't care about them.They stay surface level.You can't go deep on social media that deep.People need to invest in you, in order to care about you or to pay more attention to you.People aren't on social media to adopt new habits.They're there to entertain in themselves.When they buy a product, they're investing in their behavior change.I guarantee if you were to post a course worth of content on YouTube,you'd probably get a lot of views, because people feel like they're learning something,but they don't do anything about it because they don't value that it's free.If you were to put that inside of a product, and just make content about something similar to it,and feel the product, not only would you make a bit more money, which is great,but you'd actually impact the other person, more because they're serious about going through it. Then, since you impact more people, more people trust you, and see you as an authority,and then more people like and follow you.Social media growth isn't only about content or networking,it's also about having a product that changes lives.The second thing with having a product is it gives you a frame.It gives you a pillar to return to when writing content.So if you don't have a product, you're, probably jumping between a bunch of different interests and ideas.And there's nothing wrong with that. But you have no anchor.So when you don't know what to write,you don't have an offer to start writing around.You don't have a budget thing to build authority or expertise in.And yes, these things can change.You can go and launch a new product and start talking about something else,but you're not understanding how authority is built.That third thing there and how it helps you not quit,is that it makes trial and error more tolerable.I'm a firm believer that you should launch a product now ASAP. Immediately I launch my first at 500 followers.If you don't know what to create, watch the next video on creating a product.Because with a product, if something isn't working, you know what isn't working.You can identify.If you aren't getting clicks, then your content sucks.If you aren't getting conversions, then your landing page sucks.If you aren't making sales, even after optimizing those things,your product probably sucks and you need to make it better.And that's fine.You're supposed to start off in this embarrassing state,then iterate and improve with time.And because there's the potential to make an income, from being a creator and not getting so disappointed, because you make $0.05 from YouTube AdSense, you actually have the opportunity, to make a decent amount of money if you learn and do things right.So it's easier to stick it out when someone can actually pay you.

sections.title: 6) Build A World, Not A Funnel
sections.content: Now to help with this, let's go to step six for escaping, beginner help, which is to build a world, not a funnel.Because gone are the days of countdown timers and fake scarcity.Today, creators are building worlds, not funnels.Although a simple newsletter and a landing page can be considered a funnel.In that case, you're building a world of mini funnels.The average consumer doesn't trust people out of the gate.You need both time in the game and enough value for them to explore.In order for them to gain trust in you,you need to build a small scale marvel Cinematic Universe.You need to build movies, action figures, and law that people can binge.In other words, you need short form content.So on LinkedIn, Instagram threads, or TikTok, pick one.You need long form content.So a newsletter, podcast or YouTube, I'd recommend a newsletter first, because you'll need that anyways.And then you need free guides.So ebooks, templates, video trainings,anything else that people can show interest in. Then you need a low ticket product.So a course template or cohort you also need a high ticket product.So coaching, freelancing consulting.If you want the basics of starting with all of this,I would recommend either, the one person business Launchpad or Mental Monetization.One person Business Launchpad is for beginners, who just want to start writing content, write their first ten, post, guide it, and create their first what I call Minimum Viable offer.If you want more advanced, more low ticket product, get mental monetization.And that's like a marketing masterclass.Everything you need to know.What I would recommend doing is start, with one short form platform like X or LinkedIn or threads.Writing is a lot more accessible, easier to start.You don't need to know video editing or any of that.You just write out your ideas.Then one newsletter, same thing, just writing.So that way you have long form to build, trust and nurture and you have short form to attract traffic, and feed the newsletter. Then. Then in between the two, you have a free download for people to get on to the email list, and you send them that.It's a template, it's a free guide.It's something that helps them.That's like how you build authority around,one topic or not even one topic, but just in general.The free download is kind of like you planning a flag of, hey,this is what I'm teaching, and then everything else can revolve around that.The other thing here is that social posts are the new MVP's, so post the bunch,turn the best into newsletters, cross post those everywhere,turn the best of the best into a digital product.Refine it until sales are high.Turn that system into a software or compliment it with a physical product.My two hour rider course turned into cortex.Not exactly, but it allows me to do my workflow better in that software.It's not the education part of it, it's just it's sprung the idea.

sections.title: 7) Make Noise, Double Down On Signal
sections.content: Now, the last step,step number seven to escaping beginner, health is to make noise and double down on signal.Because the last objection here is.But Dan, I want to write about my ideas in an authentic way.This whole social media thing feels so soulless and templated and I agree.But to handle the first part, templates and frameworks, don't make you inauthentic.Creativity thrives with constraints, and a framework allows you, to express your authentic ideas in a way that other people care about.If you want to be purely authentic, without regard for what others, think, that's great, but good luck being able to sustain, that as a lifestyle that pays you,not that money is the end all, be all.But it is important, and it's only as evil as the person who wields it.Now for the second part.You can write about whatever you want.You don't have to fall into the trap, of only writing content that performs an algorithm.That's the entire point of me. creating this video is that you don't have to be a slave to the algorithm.You can do what you want if you understand what you're doing, and can make it out of beginner hell, you should make room, for both art and business templated frameworks and whatever, the ideas that are based around your offer and the ideas you like writing about.So feel free to make a lot of noise.Write about any idea your heart desires.Pay attention to the ideas that people resonate with,the ones that get slightly more engagement DM's or followers,then incorporate them as a consistent part of your content strategy.You don't need to choose a niche.You need to experiment with ideas until people tell you what, niche best suits your relationship with your audience.If you want to learn more about that,go watch my videos on the most profitable niches.You or you are the niche.That's it for this video.Again, start using cortex.Save your ideas there. Write your content there.Write any other project. Plan your days there to a writer to learn a high income skill.One person business launchpad for the fundamentals of all that stuff.Other links in the description.Check them out like subscribe,say hi in the comments and if you made it this far,I want you to answer in the comments if you want.What was the one thing that kept you trapped, or is keeping you trapped in beginner help?Because once you become aware of that, and make that public, it's a lot easier to overcome.So what is your sticking point?And if you say you have none,you're lying or you just don't have the knowledge to know better.That's it by. --- 由 vCaptions 生成 ---
